metadata.description: Lateral knee nerve at risk in tibial plateau and proximal fibular injuries.
Talocalcaneal articulation governing hindfoot alignment after calcaneus and talus injuries.
Tibiotalar and subtalar articular bone with limited vascular supply.
Bony pelvic ring formed by ilium, ischium, pubis, and sacrum.
Hindfoot bone articulating with talus at the subtalar joint.

metadata.claim_text: Treating a displaced patella fracture with extensor lag nonoperatively without operative discussion.

metadata.description: Inferior patella malposition reducing extensor mechanism efficiency after injury or surgery.
Plantar fascia and soft-tissue envelope relevant to calcaneus fracture swelling and wound risk.
Femoral shaft between proximal and distal metaphyseal regions.
Sesamoid bone within the extensor mechanism at the anterior knee.
Proximal tibial articular surface bearing medial and lateral condylar load.
Primary knee stabilizer commonly injured with tibial plateau and knee trauma.
Anterior midline pelvic articulation; disruption indicates pelvic ring instability.
Quadriceps-patella-tibial tubercle linkage enabling knee extension.
Posterior knee stabilizer relevant in high-energy knee and plateau injuries.
Major knee-region artery at risk in tibial plateau, distal femur, and knee dislocation trauma.
Central foot region including navicular, cuneiforms, and cuboid linking hindfoot to forefoot.
Open reduction internal fixation of displaced patella fracture.
Cerclage wire tension band technique for patella fracture fixation.
Distal femoral metaphysis and condylar region forming the proximal knee.

metadata.action: Immobilize in extension with progressive range-of-motion protocol when healing permits
metadata.trigger: Nondisplaced patella fracture with intact extensor mechanism and acceptable alignment

metadata.claim_text: Patella fracture management centers on extensor mechanism continuity and restoration of active knee extension.
A patient unable to perform straight-leg raise after patella fracture has extensor mechanism disruption until proven otherwise.
Describe fracture pattern, extensor exam including straight-leg raise, fixation method, and patella alta-baja awareness.

metadata.action: Discuss patella ORIF with tension band fixation per attending; document extensor exam
metadata.trigger: Displaced patella fracture with extensor mechanism disruption or inability to extend the knee

metadata.description: Posterior pelvic articulation between sacrum and ilium; key pelvic ring stability structure.
Posterior pelvic anchor articulating with ilia at the sacroiliac joints.
Tarsometatarsal articulation complex; instability defines Lisfranc injury.
Hip socket formed by ilium, ischium, and pubis; injured in acetabular fractures.
Fracture of the patella with extensor mechanism continuity concerns.
Loss of active knee extension after patella fracture displacement or failed healing.
Lateral pelvic ilium region relevant to acetabular column and pelvic ring injuries.

metadata.description: Composite pelvis-to-foot model linking pelvic ring, knee, leg, and hindfoot structures for trauma neighborhoods.

metadata.claim_text: Tension band fixation converts anterior patellar tensile forces into compression at the fracture site via the extensor mechanism.
